title: Matelea atrocoronata
intro: Matelea atrocoronata, also known as Black Swallow Wort, is a species of flowering plant in the Apocynaceae family, specifically within the genus Matelea. Traditionally, Matelea atrocoronata has been used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The bioactive constituents of Matelea atrocoronata include flavonoids, alkaloids, and glycosides, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. Matelea atrocoronata is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are commonly used to treat fever, skin irritations, and respiratory issues.

This family is further divided into several subfamilies, and Matelea atrocoronata is specifically placed within the Apocynoideae subfamily. The Apocynaceae family is a large and diverse group of plants, many of which are native to the Americas. The Apocynoideae subfamily is characterized by its unique flowers and fruit structures.
